name: Donkey Calf Raise
resistance_profile: Hip-flexed, bent-over position places the gastrocnemius on greater stretch; hardest at the bottom.
cues: Hinge at the hips and keep the back flat
Chase a deep stretch, then a full contraction
Keep the knees straight to hold tension on the gastrocnemius
execution_steps: Bend forward at the hips to roughly 90 degrees and support your upper body on the machine pad.
Place the balls of your feet on the platform with heels free, and settle the load pad across your hips.
Let your heels sink below the platform into a full calf stretch.
Press up onto your toes to full plantarflexion, keeping the knees nearly straight.
Pause at the top, then lower slowly back to the stretch.
good_when: You want maximal gastrocnemius stretch under load
You've plateaued on upright standing calf raises and want a fresh stimulus
bad_when: You have lower-back issues aggravated by the hip-flexed, loaded position
No donkey machine or suitable hip-loading setup is available
common_errors: Rounding the lower back under the load
Bouncing out of the bottom instead of stretching
progressions: Add load to the hip pad
Slow the eccentric and pause in the stretch
